title: Critical: Weak KDF Enables Offline Brute-Force Attacks
author: pascscha
body:
This implementation is affected by a critical cryptographic weakness in the Shadowsocks protocol that enables passive attackers to perform offline brute-force attacks against recorded traffic.

I have created an issue with full details at https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-org/issues/297

title: Where can I set "protocol= tunnel and local port? I want to tunnel wireguard
author: xtc0r
body:
Where does the UI save it's configuration?
I want to add some parameters to hopefully make a wireguard tunnel work but some needed parameters are not exposed in the GUI. 

Currently I get
`ERROR: [udp] invalid header with addr type 0` on my ss-local when I try to connect wireguard on port 1086.

The suggested client config from [Issue 785](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/issues/785) of the shadowsocks-rust repo is below. I hope to make it work with it.

```
"server": "server_IP",
	"server_port": 21638,
	"password": "pass",
	"method": "aes-256-gcm",
	"locals": [
		{
			"mode": "udp_only",
			"protocol": "tunnel",
			"forward_address": "server_IP",
			"forward_port": 60820,
			"local_address": "127.0.0.1",
			"local_port": 1080
                }
	]
}
```

title: Add Local Network Access Permission for MacOS 15.x
author: MidCheck
body: MacOS 15 adds local network permission access restrictions. If this permission is not assigned, the listening port cannot be accessed from the intranet. Can this permission be added? Or is there any way to put ShadowSocksX-NG in: Settings -> Privacy and Security -> Local Network I found this configuration in Cursor's info.plist:

title: Signing the .dmg for MacOS
author: SimplyYourAverageDev
body:
Sometimes when first-time users download the dmg file for MacOS, they have to go through a long process of allowing their Mac to run the application, would be great if we just signed it.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
We could try and sign the .dmg file for free if we register it with the [Fee Waiver Program](https://developer.apple.com/support/fee-waiver/) at apple.

title: Use launch_activate_socket in plist
author: liudongmiao
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Use launch_activate_socket for dynamic socket in macos.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
```xml
        <key>Sockets</key>
        <dict>
                <key>listen_key</key>
                <dict>
                        <key>SockNodeName</key>
                        <string>0.0.0.0</string>
                        <key>SockServiceName</key>
                        <string>listen_port</string>
                        <key>SockType</key>
                        <string>stream</string>
                </dict>
        </dict>
```

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
None.

**Additional context**
None

shadowsocks-libev support this features since 2.6.0, in commit https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-libev/commit/d2b7e6702dd6f0f7c2c587b5cfbbbc0a42165899, and use a magic listener name `Listeners` (pr by me :)

shadowsocks-rust support this features since latest 1.18.2, previous is buggy before https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/commit/dcdeca032cad409d1ad133abeafa0270bb32a945, use `TCPListener` in locals[0], and you can wait for next version to use it in outer config since https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/commit/7b4a0a14635e82f9bed4abed56863274a7cdfb37 (pr by me :)
